text.title: GitHub Actions CI/CD Hardening Failure Modes
text.summary: 4 antipatterns observed in real github-actions-cicd deployments, each paired with symptom / root-cause / fix.

antipattern.symptom: Build pipeline silently picks up a malicious release of a third-party action.
antipattern.root-cause: Action pinned to @v4 or @main, not a SHA.
antipattern.fix: Pin every third-party action to a full 40-character commit SHA; review SHA bumps as code.

antipattern.symptom: A compromised job pushes to the repo because GITHUB_TOKEN had write contents scope.
antipattern.root-cause: permissions: not set at workflow / job level; default is too permissive.
antipattern.fix: Set permissions: contents: read at top of workflow; grant write scopes only on the jobs that need them.

antipattern.symptom: Cloud breach traced back to an AWS access key stored as a repo secret.
antipattern.root-cause: Secrets stored as long-lived credentials instead of OIDC.
antipattern.fix: Configure cloud OIDC trust + id-token: write; mint short-lived credentials per run.

antipattern.symptom: Untrusted fork PR runs in pull_request_target context with access to secrets.
antipattern.root-cause: pull_request_target combined with checkout of fork code without trust gate.
antipattern.fix: Gate on label / member / signed commit; never checkout fork code into a context that has secrets.
